title: How I Got My Career in Foreign Policy: Stephanie Psaki
summary: Stephanie Psaki has always believed that health is at the heart of every stage of life. She sat down with CFR to talk about her far-reaching career in international research, nonprofits, and the government, as well as her views on the current state of the public health space.

title: He Can Run, but Can He Straddle?
summary: With an expected presidential run on the horizon, California Governor Gavin Newsom tries to portray himself as a tempered leader of a nation-state while also offering shape-shifting lip service to his party’s restless socialist wing. To paraphrase Lincoln: can a Newsom divided against himself stand . . . for national office?

title: How better climate diplomacy can save Europe’s green ambitions
summary: The EU needs to align climate diplomacy with industrial strategy to accelerate Europe’s transition to a competitive low-carbon economy

title: People are watching the Reflecting Pool like reality TV. What does that say about us?
summary: It's not just watching paint dry. The twists and turns of the Reflecting Pool repairs, originally a two-week project, have kept bloggers and viewers busy all summer.

title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defends Iran war funding request at fiery hearing
summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced a barrage of questions about the war with Iran from lawmakers on Tuesday, as the administration seeks more money for the escalating conflict.

title: Sen. Patty Murray, D-Wash., on Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's Senate testimony
summary: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Sen. Patty Murray of Washington about the administration's Iran war objectives following the testimony of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

title: States want to bring Medicaid behind bars. Federal changes make that harder
summary: Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.

title: Trump has campaigned more so far in 2026 midterms than he did at this point in 2018
summary: Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.

title: WATCH LIVE: House panel examines Delaney Hall ICE detention center conditions and protests
summary: The House Judiciary Oversight Subcommittee is set to hold a hearing Wednesday about the conditions at the Delaney Hall ICE detention center in Newark, New Jersey. Since late May, law enforcement has clashed outside the center with protestors who say they are demonstrating in solidarity with the detainees who have alleged poor living conditions.

title: WATCH LIVE: Trump promotes investment accounts for kids at Georgia high school
summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday plans to promote Trump Accounts, which offer $1,000 in seed money to every child born during his second term. Some parents who have signed up say they're still waiting for the money to arrive.

title: Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro back to court for pretrial hearing in his U.S. drug trafficking case
summary: Maduro, 63, and his wife Cilia Flores, 69, have been held at a Brooklyn jail ever since U.S. forces seized them from their Caracas home in a stunning middle-of-the-night raid and brought them to New York in early January.

title: China's strategic petroleum reserve as a geoeconomic tool

summary: The Emergency Medical Treatment and Active Labor Act (EMTALA, largely codified in Section 1867 of the Social Security Act, 42 U.S.C. § 1395dd) is a federal law that generally compels Medicare-participating hospitals to provide emergency care to any individual, irrespective of an individual’s ability to pay. title: Living with Wet Basements
summary: This report presents findings from a study conducted in Pittsburgh to document the prevalence of and experiences with basement wetness, the barriers that residents face in addressing it, and options available to solve the problem.

title: Examining the Impact of Senate Bill 1160 on Utilization Review and Medical Treatment in California Workers' Compensation
summary: Senate Bill 1160 reduced utilization review (UR) requirements for some treatments during the first 30 days after a work-related injury. The authors examined effects on UR processes and medical treatment in California’s workers’ compensation system.

title: Securing AI Algorithmic Insights
summary: In this report, the authors extend a security framework to provide the first systematic roadmap for protecting algorithmic insights, the novel techniques, methods, and design know-how that materially improve artificial intelligence systems.

title: Restricting AI Agent Use of Biological Tools
summary: AI agents can help non-experts use advanced biological design tools, raising misuse concerns. The authors explore safeguards built into biological tools that could block such assistance and find that their effectiveness is currently limited.
